exemplar.title:
Is replicability necessary in the production of knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of
knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title:
Is replicability necessary in the production of knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of
knowledge.

strengths: Clear and precise definitions of key terms (replicability, reliability, validity) that ground the discussion.
Use of reputable and varied sources (e.g., National Academies, Bandura DOI) to support theoretical claims.
Inclusion of well-known psychological studies (Asch, Rosenhan, Bandura) showing effort to engage with concrete examples.
Recognition of an ethical counter-perspective, indicating some awareness of alternative viewpoints.
Overall structure is coherent, with a sustained focus on the title throughout most of the essay.
weaknesses: The treatment of a second area of knowledge is perfunctory, limiting cross-AOK comparison and depth.
Analysis remains largely descriptive; links between examples and the necessity of replicability are sketched rather than fully developed.
Evaluation of counter-perspectives is mentioned but lacks depth and critical engagement.
Several bibliographic inconsistencies (missing details, inappropriate sources) undermine academic rigor.
Some methodological inaccuracies (e.g., reversed variables in Aschâs study) suggest lapses in precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the official examinerâs total is fixed at 7, the strand assessment places this essay in the mid-range. The work demonstrates clear conceptual understanding through well-chosen definitions and reputable sources, and it maintains a coherent structure and focus on replicability. It also shows some evaluation of alternative viewpoints and ethical implications. However, the analysis is largely descriptive and confined to one area of knowledge, with limited cross-AOK treatment and underdeveloped evaluation. On balance, these strengths and weaknesses align with the 5â6 band descriptors, and awarding a 7 out of 10 reflects the essayâs solid grounding in examples and theory while acknowledging its analytical and methodological shortcomings.

annotations.comment: Consider consolidating the prescribed title into one clear line to foreground the requirement to discuss two areas of knowledge explicitly.
The split between the question and the header disrupts readability; merge the title into a single header to clarify the essay prompt.
Strong definitions of replicability, reliability, and validity are provided. Ensure you integrate these definitions directly with your thesis to maintain focus.
While definitions are clear, this paragraph remains largely descriptive. Analyse how these concepts underpin whether replicability is necessary rather than just stating them.
Discussion so far is confined to psychology (human sciences). Introduce a second area of knowledge early to satisfy the task requirement.
The Asch experiment is described in detail, but you should critically evaluate how this example demonstrates that replicability is essential rather than only narrating the procedure.
The independent and dependent variables in Aschâs study are reversed here. Correct this to demonstrate accurate methodological understanding.
Link Aschâs high internal validity more clearly to your thesis on the necessity of replicability. The connection to the title remains implicit.
Good introduction to Rosenhanâs study. Next, summarise its key findings before evaluating their implications for replicability in psychology.
Rosenhanâs discussion effectively shows reliability in diagnosis, but you need to analyse how widespread misdiagnosis undermines the validity of psychiatric knowledge.
Insightful point on how ethics constrain replication. Expand this by examining how ethical frameworks shape knowledge production in another area of knowledge.
Good exposition of Banduraâs ethical issues. To deepen analysis, propose specific alternative methodologies that respect ethics yet allow replication.
You note the ethical obstacles but donât tie them back to whether replicability is strictly necessary. Clarify how ethics specifically influences the necessity of replication.
The conclusion briefly invokes artistic expression but fails to develop it. Fully integrate the arts as a second AOK or remove this mention for coherence.
The summary reiterates points but lacks synthesis; focus on drawing implications from your analysis to strengthen the argumentâs coherence.
Analysis remains confined to human sciences with only a perfunctory arts mention. Develop balanced coverage of two AOKs for a stronger response.
Although an ethical counter-perspective is mentioned, it lacks depth. Evaluate alternative views more thoroughly to demonstrate critical thinking.
Ensure you apply consistent methodological criteria (internal/external validity) across examples to enable coherent comparison between areas of knowledge.
Format the works cited section consistently using a recognized style (e.g., MLA, APA) to improve academic presentation.
The Asch citation includes a URL but omits an access date. Include full details (access date or DOI) for web sources.
The Bandura reference includes a DOI, which is excellent. Maintain this level of detail for all journal articles.
âBirth of Venusâ appears in the bibliography but is never discussed. Either integrate this artwork in the arts AOK or remove it.
The National Academies source is reputable and effectively grounds your definitions. Excellent choice for supporting theoretical claims.
The Mingay reference is incomplete, missing volume/issue details. Provide full bibliographic information to enhance credibility.
Wikipedia is not typically acceptable as an academic source. Replace these entries with primary or peer-reviewed sources, or justify their use.
The âDay of the Deadâ citation is unrelated to the essayâs analysis. Ensure each reference directly supports your argument.
The bibliography mixes peer-reviewed and crowd-sourced references. Rebalance your sources to prioritize academic rigor.

strands.content: The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

strands.content: The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

strands.content: The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
